mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding household income and aggregate consumption demand, which increases expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the positive return frequency on PAYEMS expansion months does not exceed the 65.5% baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A negative three-month percent change in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates contracting manufacturing output and falling capacity utilization, which reduces earnings expectations and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return following INDPRO contractions is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rises while the federal funds rate is constrained near zero, the absence of monetary offset amplifies labor-market downside risk, increasing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 3 months while FEDFUNDS < 0.25%
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power versus UNRATE alone (delta R-squared < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: In the post-crisis recovery, accelerating housing starts (HOUST) reflect pent-up residential investment and construction employment growth that spill over into durable goods demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts remained below long-run average in early 2014
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with below-trend housing stock
failure_condition: HOUST growth is not positively correlated with 60-day forward returns during 2011-2014 walk-forward (Rank IC < 0.03), or signal underperforms buy-and-hold in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) larger than 0.2 percent signals upside inflation risk that tightens financial conditions through rising real yields, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Taper tantrum in 2013 showed sensitivity to inflation surprises
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 55% of high-inflation-print months, or the mean return on trigger days is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter annualized real GDP growth (GDPC1) exceeding 2.5 percent indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate top-line expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline during above-trend GDP growth quarters (t-test p > 0.05), or positive return frequency falls below 65%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) confirm broad-based consumer demand strength that lowers recession probability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances, or the interaction term does not improve predictive power over PAYEMS alone (delta R-squared < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 6.5 percent signals that the Federal Reserve will delay rate increases under its forward guidance, extending the period of low discount rates and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed forward guidance in Dec 2013 cited 6.5% unemployment threshold
regime_hint: zero lower bound with forward guidance
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of months when UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, or returns during these months do not significantly exceed those when UNRATE is at or below 6.5%.
